description: Dependency graph for all Sudoku solving techniques. Each technique lists its prerequisites — board features, candidate states, and sub-techniques it relies on.

last_blank_cell.description: Finds the single empty cell in a house; computes missing digit via set difference of givens.

full_house.description: Scans each house for exactly one empty cell; the missing digit is forced.

naked_single.description: A cell with exactly one candidate remaining must be that digit.

hidden_single.description: A digit that appears as a candidate in exactly one cell of a house must go there.

pointing_pair.description: When a candidate in a block is restricted to a single row/col, eliminate from rest of that row/col.

pointing_triple.description: Three cells of a candidate in a block restricted to a single row/col.

box_line_reduction.description: When a candidate in a row/col is restricted to a single block, eliminate from rest of that block.

naked_pair.description: Two cells in a house with identical 2-candidate sets; eliminate those candidates from all other cells in the house.

hidden_pair.description: Two candidates restricted to the same two cells in a house.

naked_triple.description: Three cells whose candidate sets collectively contain ≤3 unique candidates.

hidden_triple.description: Three candidates restricted to the same three cells in a house.

naked_quad.description: Four cells whose candidate sets collectively contain ≤4 unique candidates.

hidden_quad.description: Four candidates restricted to the same four cells in a house.

xwing.description: A digit appears in exactly two rows in two columns, forming a rectangle; eliminate from intersecting columns.

swordfish.description: Three rows where a digit appears in at most three common columns (or vice versa).

jellyfish.description: Four rows where a digit appears in at most four common columns (or vice versa).

xywing.description: A bivalue pivot cell (xy) with two bivalue wings (xz, yz) that see the pivot; eliminates z from cells seen by both wings.

xyzwing.description: A trivalue pivot (xyz) with two bivalue wings (xz, yz); eliminates z from cells seen by pivot and both wings.

wwing.description: Two bivalue cells with the same pair (ab) connected by a strong link on a in a house; eliminates b from cells seen by both.

mwing.description: A bivalue cell (ab) and a strong link on a in a house, with a cell containing a as candidate seen by both.

swing.description: A bivalue cell (ab) connected to another bivalue cell (ac) via a third cell (bc) that sees both.

lwing.description: Two identical bivalue cells (ab) sharing a house, with a branching cell containing a or b.

hwing.description: Two bivalue cells sharing one candidate, with a third cell connecting them.

wxyzwing.description: A trivalue pivot (wxyz) with three bivalue wings; eliminates z from overlap.

vwxyzwing.description: A quadvalue pivot (vwxyz) with four bivalue wings; eliminates z from overlap.

unique_rectangle.description: Four cells forming a rectangle across two boxes where a deadly pattern would occur; Types 1-4 handle different candidate configurations.

hidden_rectangle.description: A UR variant where extra candidates on the rectangle cells allow elimination.

avoidable_rectangle.description: A UR variant that considers already-placed values as part of the deadly pattern.

bug.description: BUG+1: every unsolved cell except one is bivalue; the extra candidate in the non-bivalue cell is the solution.

simple_coloring.description: Color alternating strong links for a single digit; candidates of same color that see each other create conflicts.

xcycle.description: X-Cycles: closed alternating chains of strong and weak links for a single digit.

remote_pairs.description: A chain of bivalue cells where alternating links share candidates; eliminates the common candidates from cells seen by both ends.

xychain.description: A chain of bivalue cells linked by shared candidates; the start and end cells share a candidate that can be eliminated from their peers.

aic.description: Alternating Inference Chains: alternating strong and weak links between candidates across multiple digits.

alsxz.description: Two Almost Locked Sets sharing a Restricted Common (X); digits seen by both ALS (Z) can be eliminated.

finned_fish.description: Fish (X-Wing/Swordfish/Jellyfish) with an extra fin cell in a block that prevents normal elimination.

finned_xwing.description: Finned X-Wing: the base fish is an X-Wing; the fin breaks the pattern but allows elimination from the fin's block.

finned_swordfish.description: Finned Swordfish: the base fish is a Swordfish; the fin blocks complete coverage.

finned_jellyfish.description: Finned Jellyfish: the base fish is a Jellyfish; the fin blocks complete coverage.

empty_rectangle.description: A box with a candidate forming an L-shape (empty rectangle) combined with a strong link on the same candidate in a line.

alsxywing.description: Three ALS sets where two share a restricted common with the third (ALS-XY-Wing). [STUB: not implemented]

sashimi_fish.description: A Sashimi Fish is a Finned Fish where the fin cell(s) are not in the same block as the elimination target. [STUB: not implemented]

sue_de_coq.description: A cell with N candidates in a box intersecting a row/col, combined with two ALS sets. [STUB: not implemented]

extended_rectangle.description: A larger deadly pattern involving more than 4 cells in 2+ boxes. [EXPERIMENTAL]

borescope_grid.description: A grid analysis technique for detecting potential deadly patterns. [EXPERIMENTAL]

qwing.description: A wing pattern related to Unique Rectangles and bivalue cells. [EXPERIMENTAL]

gurth_symmetry.description: Exploits givens symmetry (180°, reflection) to deduce placements. [EXPERIMENTAL]

twinned_xychain.description: Two simultaneous XY-Chains sharing common endpoints. [EXPERIMENTAL]

grouped_aic.description: AIC where nodes can be groups of cells (e.g., all candidates of a digit in a house). [EXPERIMENTAL]

continuous_loop.description: A closed AIC loop where all weak links become strong. [EXPERIMENTAL]

medusa3d.description: 3D Medusa: extended coloring across multiple digits. [EXPERIMENTAL]

alschain.description: A chain of ALS sets linked by Restricted Commons. [EXPERIMENTAL]

death_blossom.description: A stem cell connected to multiple ALS sets. [EXPERIMENTAL]

franken_fish.description: Fish using mixed row/col/box cover sets. [EXPERIMENTAL]

mutant_fish.description: Fish with non-standard cover sets mixing rows, cols, and boxes. [EXPERIMENTAL]

siamese_fish.description: Two overlapping fish patterns sharing base or cover sets. [EXPERIMENTAL]

aligned_pair_exclusion.description: APE: two cells that see each other; value pairs that would make the puzzle invalid can be eliminated. [EXPERIMENTAL — uses clone+place]

pattern_overlay.description: POM: exhaustive template intersection for each digit. [EXPERIMENTAL — uses backtracking]

forcing_chains.description: Forcing Chains: assume a candidate and follow inferences to a common conclusion or contradiction. [EXPERIMENTAL — uses clone+place]

nishio.description: Nishio: for a single digit, test if placing it in a cell leads to a contradiction. [EXPERIMENTAL — delegates to ForcingChains]

bowmans_bingo.description: Bowman's Bingo: select a cell, try a value, propagate, and check for contradiction. [EXPERIMENTAL — uses 2-level clone+try]

extended_sue_de_coq.description: Sue de Coq extended to larger intersections. [EXPERIMENTAL]

fireworks.description: A firework pattern: a candidate appears in exactly one cell outside a box intersection. [EXPERIMENTAL]

guardians.description: Guardians (also called Broken Wings): extra candidates preventing a fish pattern. [EXPERIMENTAL]

tridagon.description: A Triangular Deadly Pattern involving 3 digits in 3 cells across 3 boxes. [EXPERIMENTAL]

skloop.description: An SK Loop is a pattern of four ALS pairs forming a loop around the grid. [EXPERIMENTAL]

exocet.description: An Exocet: a base cell(s) whose candidates are locked in target cells across the grid. [EXPERIMENTAL]

double_exocet.description: Double version of Exocet with two base sets and two target sets. [EXPERIMENTAL]
